toolbox_labels.软件 I2C/自定义引脚（兼容硬件 I2C）：使用 SDA/SCL 输入: Software I2C/custom pins (hardware-I2C compatible): enter SDA/SCL

tm1650_init_hardware.message0: hardware I2C initialize TM1650 %1 digits %2 brightness %3
tm1650_init_hardware.tooltip: Initialize TM1650 with the board's default hardware I2C SDA/SCL pins; do not also use custom-pin initialization

tm1650_init.message0: software I2C/custom-pin initialize TM1650 %1 digits %2 SDA %3 SCL %4 brightness %5
tm1650_init.tooltip: Use custom SDA/SCL on ESP32/ESP8266; other hardware-I2C boards automatically fall back to default Wire pins for compile compatibility

tm1650_show_text.tooltip: Show digits or supported letters; a decimal point attaches to the preceding character without using a position

tm1650_show_number.tooltip: Format a number with the selected decimal places and show the available leading digits

tm1650_clear.tooltip: Clear all characters and decimal points while keeping the display enabled

tm1650_set_dot.tooltip: Set a decimal point using positions counted from the left; invalid positions are safely ignored

tm1650_set_brightness.tooltip: Set brightness from 0 to 7; gradual mode takes about 50 ms per level

tm1650_set_power.tooltip: Enable or disable display output while retaining the display buffer

tm1650_scroll_start.tooltip: Show the first scrolling frame and return the number of shifts remaining

tm1650_scroll_next.tooltip: Shift left by one frame and return the number of shifts remaining

tm1650_scroll_text.message0: TM1650 %1 scroll all text %2 every %3 ms
tm1650_scroll_text.tooltip: Run a complete blocking scroll; use start and next-frame blocks for responsive programs

tm1650_set_segments.tooltip: Write a raw seven-segment byte from 0 to 255 to a position

tm1650_set_control.tooltip: Write a raw value to a TM1650 position control register

tm1650_get_segments.tooltip: Read the current segment code for a position; invalid positions return 0

tm1650_get_brightness.tooltip: Return the current brightness from 0 to 7

tm1650_get_digits.tooltip: Return the number of display positions selected during initialization
